variants.title: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the tongue smell behind this animal behavior: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.seo_title: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.yt_description: Snakes smell the air with their tongues. Watch the tongue smell first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick.
variants.script: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick. Watch the tongue flick, because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth. That helps snakes track prey, mates, and danger without needing a loud chase. The air is information. Would you notice the trail?
variants.lead: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick. Watch the tongue flick, because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth. That helps snakes track prey, mates, and danger without needing a loud chase. The air is information. Would you notice the trail?

variants.title: Owls fly silently because feathers break sound
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the silent feathers behind this animal behavior: Owls can make a hunt almost silent
variants.seo_title: Owls fly silently because feathers break sound
variants.yt_description: Owls fly silently because feathers break sound. Watch the silent feathers first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Owls can make a hunt almost silent.
variants.script: Owls can make a hunt almost silent. Watch the wing edge, because owl feathers have soft fringes that break up noisy airflow. That quiet flight helps them hear prey and arrive without warning. The silence is built into the feather. Would you hear the landing?

variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the cloud spiral behind this animal behavior: Storm clouds filter sunlight into a softer glow
variants.seo_title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
variants.yt_description: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine. Watch the cloud spiral first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Storm clouds show where a storm is feeding.
variants.script: Storm clouds show where a storm is feeding. Watch the spiral shape, because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build. Rotation organizes that energy into bands. From above, the storm is not random; it is an engine. Which satellite clue next?

variants.seo_title: Lightning makes air explode into thunder

variants.hook: Lightning turns air into a shock wave.
variants.script: Lightning turns air into a shock wave. Watch the flash first, because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast. That air expands suddenly, and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder. The sound is the atmosphere snapping back. Which storm signal next?
